header.title: Regex Tester — Test & Debug Regular Expressions Online Free
header.description: Free online regex tester to test and debug regular expressions with live matching. Visualize pattern matches, test replacements, and explore regex syntax with detailed explanations. Supports flags, groups, lookaheads, and backreferences.
header.keywords: regex tester, regular expression tester, regex debugger, test regex online, regex matcher, regex pattern tester, regular expression checker, regex tool

enterTestText: Enter test text on the left
captureGroups: No capture groups in pattern — use (group) syntax
enableFlag: Enable the d flag to see group indices
replacementTip: use $1, $2 for groups, $& for full match

howToUse.title: How to Use the Regex Tester
howToUse.step1Desc: Type or paste a regular expression pattern into the Pattern field.
howToUse.step2Desc: Toggle flags like Global, Case Insensitive, and Multiline to control matching behavior.
howToUse.step3Desc: Paste the text you want to test against in the Test String area.
howToUse.step4Desc: View highlighted matches, capture groups, and use the Replace tab to test substitutions.

items.question: What regex engine does this tool use?
items.answer: It uses JavaScript's built-in RegExp engine, supporting standard ECMAScript regex syntax.

items.question: Can I test regex replacement?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to the Replace tab, enter a replacement pattern, and see the result instantly.

items.question: What do the flags mean?
items.answer: Flags modify how the pattern matches: g (global), i (case insensitive), m (multiline), s (dotAll), u (unicode), y (sticky).

items.question: How do I reference captured groups in replacement?
items.answer: Use $1, $2, etc. for numbered groups, $& for the full match, and $` or $' for text before or after the match.

examples.title: Example Results
examples.sampleText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
